description: Figure 8 presents a correlative ECCI and KAM-EBSD analysis of geometrically necessary boundary (GNB) structures in a deformed austenitic FeMnAlC low-density steel. Panels (a) and (b) show ECC images of a Type 1a cell block (CB) formed by a single GNB system (GNB1), where panel (b) resolves dense dislocation walls (DDWs) and lenticular microbands (MB1a, MB1b) with measured thicknesses of 88 nm, 150 nm, and 190 nm, respectively. Panel (c) provides the corresponding KAM-EBSD map quantifying local boundary misorientations of θ^H_DDW1a = 1.0 ± 0.1°, θ^H_MB1a = 1.9 ± 0.1°, and θ^H_MB1b = 2.0 ± 0.2°, establishing that GNB misorientations below 2° correspond to DDWs while those above 2° are associated with microbands. Panel (d) is an ECC image of a Type 1b CB revealing localized formation of MB1a and MB1b along GNB1 sections alongside a second GNB set (GNB2). These results demonstrate quantitative criteria to distinguish DDWs from MBs based on boundary misorientation and reveal localized microband formation along GNB segments.
